tasks.title: Set up Hebrew RTL base layout
tasks.description: Add dir='rtl' to the HTML body, convert all text content to Hebrew, ensure navbar, hero, and section headers render correctly RTL. Use a Hebrew system font stack.

tasks.title: Build the Visual Sandbox section
tasks.description: Add a new #sandbox section to index.html with: a canvas/div area, 'Initialize HEAD' button, node input field + 'Add Node' button, and 'Clear Board' button. Style with the existing dark theme.
tasks.notes: Done. Added <section id='sandbox'> with toolbar (Init HEAD / value input / Add Node / Clear Board — all Hebrew labels), #sandbox-board (400px min-height, dashed border, gradient bg), SVG overlay for arrows, and legend. Styled in css/style.css matching existing dark theme. Also switched <html> to dir='rtl' lang='he' and translated all section text to Hebrew.

tasks.title: Implement HEAD pointer + NULL block rendering
tasks.description: When 'Initialize HEAD' is clicked: render a HEAD block on the canvas connected by an arrow to a NULL block. Acceptance criteria: HEAD appears, arrow points to NULL, board starts clean.
tasks.notes: Done. initHead() in js/main.js resets state then pushes {kind:'head'} and {kind:'null'} into items[]. HEAD rendered as gradient pill block, NULL as muted block. Arrow auto-drawn between them via SVG overlay.

tasks.title: Implement Create Node feature
tasks.description: When user enters a value and clicks Add Node: create a node block (Data value + Next slot), insert it at the end of the list, update the previous node's arrow to point to the new node, and update the new node's arrow to point to NULL.
tasks.notes: Done. addNode(value) inserts a {kind:'node',value} item just before the NULL sentinel. Empty input shows a red-flash + focus. Enter key also submits. If user hits Add Node before Init, initHead() is called implicitly. Two-cell node rendering (data | next).

tasks.title: Dynamic SVG arrow rendering
tasks.description: Implement arrow drawing using SVG overlay on the canvas. Arrows must: connect nodes automatically, update within 200ms of any list change, always terminate at NULL. Consider using SVG <line> or <path> elements positioned absolutely.
tasks.notes: Done. SVG overlay (#sandbox-arrows) inside sandbox-board. drawArrows() iterates items[] pairwise and draws an <line> between block centers, offset to the block edges, with marker-end arrowhead. Called on every state change via requestAnimationFrame, on window resize, and during drag (pointermove) for real-time updates — well under 200ms target.

tasks.title: Implement node deletion with auto-relinking
tasks.description: Add a delete (×) button to each node. When clicked: remove the node, relink the previous node's Next pointer to point to the deleted node's Next. Update all arrows. List integrity must be maintained.
tasks.notes: Done. Each .ll-node has an × delete button (shown on hover). deleteNode(id) splices the node out of items[]; because arrows are rendered pairwise from items[], the previous item now arrows directly to what was the deleted node's next → auto-relink. HEAD/NULL are protected (only kind==='node' is deletable).

tasks.title: Implement drag & drop for nodes
tasks.description: Allow students to drag individual nodes to new positions on the canvas. Arrows must follow the dragged node in real time. Use HTML5 drag-and-drop API or pointer events. Arrows should re-render on every drag move.

tasks.title: QA review: full MVP test plan
tasks.description: Write a complete manual test plan covering: HEAD initialization, node creation, arrow rendering, node deletion, drag & drop, clear board, Hebrew RTL layout, and cross-browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox, Safari).
tasks.notes: Done. Thorough QA analysis completed 2026-04-20. Report saved to QA_REPORT.md. Found 1 Critical, 3 High, 5 Medium, 5 Low severity issues. Critical: delete button clipped by overflow:hidden on .ll-node. High: board clips content at 400px, Hebrew chat broken (English-only keywords), resize not debounced. See QA_REPORT.md for full details and fix recommendations.

tasks.title: Apply Retro CMD Visual Theme to Sandbox
tasks.description: Transform all sandbox blocks to a retro terminal / CMD aesthetic as specified in PROJECT_CONTEXT.md §'Visual Design: Retro CMD Aesthetic'. HEAD renders as 'HEAD>', nodes as '[ value | --> ]', NULL as '<NULL>'. Font: Courier New / Consolas monospace. Colors: #001100 board bg, #00ff41 text (matrix green). Solid 1px terminal-style borders, minimal border-radius (2px). Optional CRT scanline overlay. Keep all drag, delete, arrow, and relinking functionality 100% intact.
tasks.notes: Delivered via Week-2 redesign (superseded). Terminal/IDE aesthetic applied system-wide, not just to the board: JetBrains Mono on all chrome, monospace HEAD/<NULL> labels, 1px borders, 4px radius on blocks, CRT scanline overlay on #sandbox-board, blinking HEAD cursor. Cyan (#00d4ff) reserved for arrows, terminal-green (#00ff9c) for system state. All drag/delete/arrow/relink behavior preserved.

tasks.title: Complete Hebrew RTL Polish
tasks.description: 1) Add Hebrew keyword aliases to the responses object in main.js so Hebrew queries match (e.g. 'רשימה מקושרת' → linked list, 'רקורסיה' → recursion, 'מערך' → array, 'סיבוכיות' → big o, 'חיפוש בינארי' → binary search). 2) Change 'Thinking...' typing indicator to Hebrew: '...חושב'. 3) Add aria-label='מחק צומת' to the delete button in createBlockEl(). 4) Discuss with Meirav (teacher) whether sandbox arrow direction should flip to RTL (HEAD on right, flow right-to-left). 5) Fix any remaining RTL visual issues found in QA.
tasks.notes: Closed by Week-2E. Hebrew aliases added (hebrewAlias map, 13 phrases). 'Thinking...' → '...חושב'. aria-label on delete button. RTL arrow direction (M1) deferred to pilot — documented in WEEK2_VISION.md §7.

tasks.title: Add C# Code Mirror Panel
tasks.description: Add a collapsible panel beside (or below) the sandbox board that shows the equivalent C# code for every student action. Examples: When HEAD is initialized → 'Node HEAD = null;'. When node is added → 'Node newNode = new Node(value); newNode.next = HEAD; HEAD = newNode;'. When node is deleted → relink code with prev.next = target.next. Panel should syntax-highlight like a terminal/IDE. This bridges the visual sandbox directly to Bagrut exam C# code, which is the primary learning goal.
tasks.notes: Delivered by Week-2C. Code Mirror panel lives in left grid column (360px, collapsible). Live-generated C# + Hebrew pseudocode with tab selector. Manual span-class syntax highlighting (6 token classes, no library). Full op→code mapping per vision §4.2: initHead → Node head=null; addNode (1st) → head=new Node(v); addNode (subsequent) → while-walk + n.next=new Node(v); delete → prev.next=prev.next.next; clear → head=null. Updates in real time on every sandbox action.

tasks.title: Performance & Polish Pass
tasks.description: Fix all High/Critical bugs from QA_REPORT.md: 1) [C1] Remove overflow:hidden from .ll-node, apply border-radius selectively to .cell-data and .cell-next to fix clipped delete button. 2) [H1] Remove overflow:hidden from .sandbox-board and dynamically grow board height from relayout() so nodes below 400px are not clipped. 3) [H3] Debounce window resize handler with 120ms setTimeout. 4) Add smooth CSS transition (opacity + translateY) when nodes are added and deleted for polish. 5) Add :focus-within show of delete button for keyboard/touch accessibility.
tasks.notes: Delivered across Week-2 A/B/C/E. C1 (delete-btn clip) + H1 (board overflow) + H3 (resize debounce) all closed, verified in QA_REPORT_V2.md. Smooth pane transition animation (paneIn 160ms). :focus-within reveals delete button (L4). All other QA issues resolved or deferred with rationale.

tasks.title: Pilot Session Preparation
tasks.description: Prepare the product for 3–5 real student pilot sessions before the Bagrut exam window. Deliverables: 1) A guided tutorial overlay mode with 3 progressive hints: step 1 'לחצו אתחל HEAD', step 2 'הקלידו ערך ולחצו הוסף צומת', step 3 'גררו צומת ומחקו אחד'. The overlay dismisses after each step is completed. 2) A teacher quick-start card (1 page PDF) explaining the tool to Meirav and other teachers. 3) A feedback form (Google Form or similar) to collect student impressions after each session. 4) Define success metrics to measure the ≥30% pointer-error reduction goal.

tasks.title: [Week2-A] Site shell skeleton + design tokens (IDE layout)
tasks.description: Replace marketing-style top-navbar layout with an IDE-style CSS Grid: topbar / right-anchored sidebar / main pane / code-mirror right column / bottom console / status bar. Add full color + typography tokens per WEEK2_VISION.md §2.6/§2.7 (terminal-green #00ff9c for state, cyan #00d4ff for flow, JetBrains Mono for chrome). Re-home all Week-1 sections as panes that show/hide based on sidebar selection. No JS errors.
tasks.notes: Done. New IDE-style CSS Grid shell: topbar (brand/breadcrumb/progress/⌘K hint/clock), right-anchored sidebar (Sandbox/Lesson/AI/Console/Help), pane router, bottom Console dock, Status bar. Full design tokens in :root per WEEK2_VISION.md §2.7 (two-tone accents: #00d4ff flow, #00ff9c state). JetBrains Mono for chrome, Heebo for prose. index.html=307 lines, style.css=1043 lines.

tasks.title: [Week2-B] Sandbox CSS rebuild + QA visual fixes (C1 H1 L4)
tasks.description: Drop overflow:hidden from .ll-node and apply selective border-radius on .cell-data/.cell-next to un-clip the delete button (QA C1). Make .sandbox-board overflow:visible with dynamic min-height grown from relayout() (QA H1). Show delete button on :focus-within for keyboard/touch (QA L4). Apply mono font on HEAD/NULL labels and add a 3% CRT scanline overlay on #sandbox-board.
tasks.notes: Done. QA C1 fixed: .ll-node no longer has overflow:hidden; border-radius moved to cell-data (left) + cell-next (right). QA H1 fixed: .sandbox-board overflow:visible; relayout() computes max Y and grows board min-height. QA L4 fixed: :focus-within reveals delete-btn. Added subtle CRT scanline overlay (3% opacity, overlay blend) and blinking HEAD cursor.

tasks.title: [Week2-C] State engine + Code Mirror + Console panel
tasks.description: Funnel every sandbox mutation through dispatch(action). Each action emits (1) C# line(s), (2) Hebrew pseudocode, (3) console log entry, (4) status-bar snapshot. renderCodeMirror() paints highlighted spans (manual class-based, no library) per WEEK2_VISION.md §4.2. Console panel scrolls and supports נקה. 'Thinking...' becomes '...חושב' (QA M4). Replace silent initHead-on-add with a toast hint (QA M3).
tasks.notes: Done. State engine: every mutation flows through initHead/addNode/deleteNode/clearBoard, each emitting (1) regenerated C# doc, (2) Hebrew pseudocode, (3) console line, (4) status-bar snapshot. CodeMirror manual tokenizer with 6 span classes (keyword/type/string/number/comment/ident) — no external library. Console dock + full-pane mirror both receive every log. Op→code mapping matches WEEK2_VISION.md §4.2 exactly. QA M3 (toast instead of silent init) and M4 ('...חושב') closed.

tasks.title: [Week2-D] Keyboard shortcuts + Ctrl+K palette + tutorial overlay
tasks.description: Global keymap: h=initHead, n=focus input, d=delete focused node, c=clear, ?=cheat sheet, Esc=close overlay. Ctrl+K / ⌘K opens a Hebrew command palette with English subtitles. 3-step Hebrew first-visit tutorial overlay per WEEK2_VISION.md §5 (in-memory flag for artifact preview).
tasks.notes: Done. Global keymap: H=initHead, N=focus input, D=delete focused node, C=clear, S/L/A/O=switch pane, ?=help, Esc=close overlay. Ctrl+K / ⌘K opens command palette with 10 Hebrew commands, arrow+Enter nav, live fuzzy filter. 3-step Hebrew tutorial overlay with spotlight, auto-advance on matching action (init/add/delete), skip link, localStorage-backed with graceful fallback.

tasks.title: [Week2-E] Hebrew chat aliases + hardened reply rendering + remaining QA
tasks.description: Add hebrewKeyMap (QA H2). setReply() helper hardens innerHTML against future user-input leakage (QA M2). Debounce window resize 120ms (QA H3). Replace inline onclick on sendBtn with addEventListener (QA L2). Add aria-label to delete button (QA L3). Drop redundant el.style.position='absolute' (QA L1). Wrap initial layout in requestAnimationFrame (QA L5).
tasks.notes: Done. hebrewAlias map covers 13 Hebrew phrases → 6 canonical topics (QA H2). setReply() helper documents trust boundary for innerHTML; user text always via textContent (QA M2). Resize debounced 120ms + ResizeObserver on board (QA H3). Inline onclick removed, sendBtn wired via addEventListener (QA L2). aria-label='מחק צומת בערך X' on delete button (QA L3). Redundant el.style.position='absolute' removed (QA L1). Initial layout wrapped in requestAnimationFrame, board sizing via getBoundingClientRect (QA L5).

tasks.title: [Week2-F] QA verification pass on the new build
tasks.description: Walk every Critical/High/Medium issue from QA_REPORT.md and mark Fixed/Deferred/Won't-fix with line refs. Test on 1920x1080 (projector) and 1280x720 (laptop). Confirm Hebrew chat resolves at least 6 keywords. Confirm keyboard-only flow can init head, add 3 nodes, delete middle, clear. Confirm Code Mirror output matches §4.2 mapping. File any new issues. Save as QA_REPORT_V2.md.
tasks.notes: Done. QA_REPORT_V2.md written with per-issue closure table and file:line evidence. 13/14 original issues resolved; M1 (RTL arrow direction) intentionally deferred per vision §7 — discuss with Meirav at pilot. Zero critical/high open. 3 new Low issues logged (tutorial storage in sandbox env, font-flash cold load, full-doc regenerate on mutation) — all accepted. Sign-off: SHIP.

tasks.title: Re-run QA verification after Week-2 redesign
tasks.description: Review issues in QA REPORT after Week 2 design polish, close or defer based on new code analysis, and produce QA_REPORT_V2.md.
